findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 36. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: 'legend: reservoir node, demand node, junction node, flow arc, withdrawal arc, ww...' (7x).

findings.description: Flow Chart diagram image for Upper Cape Fear Basin is missing. The PDF shows a visual diagram with shaped nodes (triangles, squares, ovals), directional arrows, and spatial layout. The HTML replaces this with a text-based bullet list of node names.
findings.evidence: PDF page 10 has a full flow chart diagram with Reidsville Dam, Old Stony Ck Reservoir, Lake Brandt, Lake Townsend, etc. connected by colored arrows (Flow Arc, Withdrawal Arc, WW Return Arc) with a legend. HTML renders only the node names as bullet points and a text legend, losing all spatial and relational information.

findings.description: Flow Chart diagram image for Middle Cape Fear Basin is missing. The visual diagram with nodes, arrows, and annotations is replaced by a text list.
findings.evidence: PDF page 11 shows a flow chart with Cane Creek Reservoir, OWASA Demand, University Lake, Stone Quarry, Jordan Lake, Pittsboro Demand, Harris Lake, etc. connected by arrows with annotation boxes. HTML shows only bullet points listing these nodes.

findings.description: Flow Chart diagram image for Deep River is missing. The visual diagram is replaced by a text list.
findings.evidence: PDF page 12 shows a flow chart with High Point Reservoir, Siler City Reservoirs, Randleman Reservoir, Ramseur Reservoir, etc. connected by colored arrows with annotation boxes. HTML shows only bullet points.

findings.description: Flow Chart diagram image for Lower Cape Fear Basin is missing. The visual diagram is replaced by a text list.
findings.evidence: PDF page 13 shows a flow chart with Flow from Upstream at Lillington, Harnett Co. Demand, Dunn Demand, Fayetteville Demand, Glenville Reservoir, Cape Fear Kelly Gage, Wilmington & Lower Cape Fear WASA Demands, Model Terminus connected by arrows. HTML shows only bullet points.

findings.description: Flow Chart diagram image for Upper Neuse Basin is missing. The visual diagram is replaced by a text list.
findings.evidence: PDF page 14 shows a flow chart with West Fork Eno Reservoir, Lake Orange, Lake Michie, Little River Reservoir, Hillsborough Demand, Durham Demand, Beaverdam Lake, Falls Lake, Raleigh Demand, Neuse Clayton Gage connected by arrows with multiple annotation boxes. HTML shows only bullet points.

findings.description: Flow Chart diagram image for Middle Neuse Basin is missing. The visual diagram is replaced by a text list.
findings.evidence: PDF page 15 shows a flow chart with Crabtree Creek Flood Impoundments, Lake Wheeler, Swift Creek, Lake Benson, Neuse Clayton Gage, Johnson Co. & Smithfield Demand, Neuse Goldsboro Gage, Goldsboro Demand connected by arrows. HTML shows only bullet points.

findings.description: Flow Chart diagram image for Lower Neuse Basin is missing. The visual diagram is replaced by a text list.
findings.evidence: PDF page 16 shows a flow chart with Buckhorn Reservoir, Contentnea Creek, Wilson Demand, Neuse Kinston Gage, Lower Neuse WASA Demand, Model Terminus connected by arrows with an annotation box. HTML shows only bullet points.

findings.description: Seven substantive flow chart diagrams (PDF pages 10–16) showing spatial relationships between nodes, connection arrows, and arc types for each basin area are missing as images from the HTML. The text annotations from each diagram are extracted into unordered bullet lists, but the actual diagram images are absent, losing the visual depiction of how nodes connect and water flows through the system.
findings.evidence:
PDF has flow chart diagrams for: Upper Cape Fear Basin (p10), Middle Cape Fear Basin (p11), Deep River (p12), Lower Cape Fear Basin (p13), Upper Neuse Basin (p14), Middle Neuse Basin (p15), Lower Neuse Basin (p16). The HTML has no tags for any of these seven diagrams; their content is rendered only as
    bullet lists.
